criterion: Quality and efficiency of the implementation

sub_sections.title: Work plan and resources
sub_sections.content: MAESTRO is structured across nine WPs in four functional layers: WP1 (Project Management and Coordination, ATU lead, M1–48); three research pillars — WP2 (Neuro-Symbolic Planning and Reasoning Engine, ATU lead, M1–36), WP3 (Adaptive Memory Architecture, BIIS lead, M1–30), WP4 (Decentralised Multi-Agent Coordination, CERIA lead, M3–36); three demonstrator WPs — WP5 (Healthcare Demonstrator, NIHS lead, M12–42), WP6 (Manufacturing Demonstrator, ISIA lead, M12–42), WP7 (Logistics Transfer Demonstrator, ELI lead, M12–42); WP8 (Integration, Evaluation and Benchmarking, FIIT lead, M6–48); and WP9 (Dissemination, Exploitation and Communication, BAL lead, M1–48). The dependency map contains 16 confirmed inter-WP data-input edges forming an acyclic graph; within this, the three research pillar WPs feed the three demonstrator WPs through 9 pillar-to-demonstrator edges; WP8 integrates outputs from all pillar and demonstrator WPs; WP9 depends on WP8 final results. The critical path runs through T2-01→T2-02→T2-03 (WP2, M1–36), T5-01→T5-02→T5-03 (WP5, M12–42), and T8-03 (WP8, M42–48), reflecting that planning engine maturity gates clinical demonstrator deployment and final benchmarking. The project delivers 20 essential deliverables across 9 WPs — including D1-02 (Data management plan, M6), D2-02 (Planning engine software prototype, M36), D8-01 (Evaluation framework and benchmark specification, M12), D8-03 (Final integration and evaluation report, M48), and D9-01 (Dissemination and exploitation plan, M6) — and 11 milestones spanning M3 to M48. Key milestones: MS1 (Architecture specification and integration contracts finalised, M3), MS6 (Integrated MAESTRO framework v1.0, M24), MS9 (Demonstrator validation complete across all three sectors, M42), MS11 (Project completion, M48). Risk management covers 11 identified risks across technical, external, and organisational categories; top risks include neuro-symbolic integration complexity (RISK-01, medium likelihood/high impact, mitigated by modular fallback design and early integration testing from M6), LLM baseline evolution (RISK-07, high/medium, mitigated by model-agnostic component interfaces), clinical data access delays (RISK-04, medium/high, mitigated by public dataset fallback including MIMIC-IV), and framework integration fragmentation (RISK-10, medium/high, mitigated by M1–M3 API contract definition and bimonthly integration checkpoints). Three ethics categories are triggered — Category 8 (AI), Category 4 (health data), Category 2 (human participants in medical context) — with ethics oversight led by ATU (Ethics Compliance Coordinator) and NIHS (Healthcare Ethics Oversight Officer) through the dedicated Ethics and Quality Oversight Committee.

sub_sections.title: Capacity of participants and consortium as a whole
sub_sections.content: The MAESTRO consortium brings together eight complementary European organisations across academic, applied research, and industry sectors, covering all three Apply AI sectors mandated by the call (SR-07). WP leadership is distributed across the consortium: ATU leads both WP1 and WP2, while BIIS, CERIA, NIHS, ISIA, ELI, FIIT, and BAL each lead one major functional WP. ATU (Alpenstadt Technical University, DE, HES) serves as coordinator and leads the neuro-symbolic planning pillar, with its Chair of AI Foundations providing world-class expertise in automated planning, LLM-based reasoning, and neuro-symbolic AI; ATU contributes to WP3, WP4, WP5, WP6, WP8, and WP9. CERIA (Centre Européen de Recherche en Informatique Avancée, FR, REC) leads WP4, contributing formal multi-agent coordination, distributed AI, and game-theoretic protocol expertise; CERIA further contributes to WP2, WP3, WP6, WP7, and WP8. BIIS (Breda Institute for Intelligent Systems, NL, HES) leads WP3, contributing deep learning, information retrieval, and memory-augmented neural network expertise as a pioneer in retrieval-augmented generation; BIIS contributes to WP2, WP4, WP5, and WP8. ISIA (Istituto Superiore di Ingegneria Avanzata, IT, HES) leads WP6, providing an operational pilot factory environment, digital twin infrastructure, and AI for manufacturing expertise; ISIA contributes to WP1, WP8, and WP9. NIHS (Nordic Institute of Health Sciences, SE, HES) leads WP5, providing de-identified clinical datasets, established clinical validation pathways, and healthcare AI research capability for the clinical decision support demonstrator; NIHS contributes to WP1 and WP8. ELI (EuroLog International AG, CH, PRC) leads WP7, supplying real-world logistics data and multi-corridor deployment environments across three international corridors; ELI contributes to WP1, WP8, and WP9. BAL (Boreal AI Labs Oy, FI, PRC) leads WP9, contributing LLM fine-tuning capability, production-grade deployment infrastructure, and AI-on-demand platform integration per SR-10 and CC-05; BAL contributes to WP2, WP3, WP5, WP6, WP7, and WP8. FIIT (Fundación Ibérica de Innovación Tecnológica, ES, REC) leads WP8, providing AI evaluation framework design, cross-demonstrator benchmarking methodology, and EDIH network dissemination capacity supporting TEF coordination per CC-07; FIIT contributes to WP5, WP6, WP7, and WP9. Administrative eligibility declarations are handled outside this B.3.2 narrative and are not repeated here unless directly required by the section schema and traceable to a declared source.

claim_statuses.claim_summary: 9-WP structure with WP leads and contributing partners as described
ATU leads both WP1 and WP2; other partners each lead one WP
16 confirmed inter-WP data-input edges forming acyclic dependency graph
9 pillar-to-demonstrator edges from WP2/WP3/WP4 to WP5/WP6/WP7
11 milestones MS1–MS11 with due months 3–48 and verifiable criteria
Budget gate passed; all 9 WPs and 8 partners covered in validated budget
Governance: General Assembly (biannual, all 8), PMC (quarterly, all 8), EQOC (ATU, NIHS)
11 risks identified with likelihood, impact, and mitigation measures
Partner descriptions and expertise as stated for all 8 partners
BAL AI-on-demand platform integration mandate per SR-10 and CC-05
SR-07 mandates coverage of Apply AI sectors including healthcare and manufacturing
FIIT TEF coordination per CC-07 (AI Factories and Testing and Experiment Facilities)
D5-02 dissemination level SEN (health data); other deliverables PU (open science)
claim_statuses.source_ref: Inferred: ethics_assessment ETHICS-02 health data→SEN; SR-10/CC-05 open science mandate→PU
